channel.title: My Daily AI Vocab Digest
channel.itunes:author: Vocab Agent
channel.description: Custom personalized daily spaced repetition audio lessons
channel.itunes:summary: Custom personalized daily spaced repetition audio lessons.

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-07-01
item.description:
Today we have four terms: bound to notice, transient, formidable, and mandane.
First, bound to notice. Can you recall what it means if someone is bound to notice something? ... ... ... ... Bound to notice means very likely to notice, almost certain to notice. You say it when something is hard to mi...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-30
item.description:
Today we have six terms: belittled, entail, attribute, rebuttal, bound to notice, and transient.
Let’s start with a review. What does belittled mean? ... ... ... ... The base word is belittle, and its root meaning is to make someone or something seem little, small, or unimportant.
Belittle (verb) ...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-29
item.description:
Today we have five terms: conjecture, endearment, predicate, succumbed, and contrition.
Before we review conjecture, can you recall what it means? ... ... ... ... Conjecture has the root idea of throwing ideas together to make a guess from incomplete facts.
Conjecture (noun) means a guess or opini...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-28
item.description:
Today we have ten terms: formidable, concede, coax, coalesce, defer, untenable, regime, ensuing, belated, and dispel.
Let’s review formidable. Can you recall what it means? ... ... ... ... Formidable has a core meaning of causing fear, difficulty, or strong respect. Formidable (adjective) describes...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-27
item.description:
Today we have fifteen terms: belittled, entail, attribute, rebuttal, agree to disagree, been on the bubble, hearsay, futile, estranged, god forbid, conflicted, superficial, profound, persuasive, and formidable.
First, belittled. Can you recall what belittled means? ... ... ... ... Belittle means to...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-26
item.description:
Today we have ten terms: conjecture, endearment, predicate, succumbed, contrition, horrendous, belittled, entail, attribute, and rebuttal.
Let’s review conjecture. Can you recall what conjecture means? ... ... ... ... Conjecture means a guess based on limited information. The root idea is joining p...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-25
item.description:
Today we have fourteen terms: concede, coax, coalesce, defer, untenable, imminent, red herring, perpetually, ubiquitous, conjecture, endearment, predicate, succumbed, and contrition.
Let’s review concede. Can you recall its meaning? ... ... ... ... concede has the root idea of giving way. concede (...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-24
item.description:
Today we have five terms: concede, coax, coalesce, defer, and untenable.
Concede means to give in, admit something is true, or accept that you have lost. The root idea is yielding after resistance. Common collocations are concede defeat and concede a point.
Concede (verb) means to admit or accept ...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-22
item.description:
Today we have three terms: imminent, red herring, and perpetually.
First, can you recall what imminent means? ... ... ... ... Imminent means very close to happening, usually something important or serious. The root idea is hanging over you, like an event about to arrive.
Imminent (adjective) means...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-21
item.description:
Today, we are learning seven terms: Regime, ensuing, belated, dispel, Imminent, red herring, and perpetually.
Let's start with our review words. Do you remember the meaning of the word Regime? ... ... ... ... Regime is a noun. It refers to a system of government or a particular period of rule. It c...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-20
item.description:
Hello and welcome. Today, we have ten interesting terms to explore: agree to disagree, been on the bubble, hearsay, futile, estranged, god forbid, conflicted, superficial, profound, and persuasive.
Let's start with a phrase we've discussed before: agree to disagree. Do you remember what it means? ....

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-19
item.description:
Today, we have one vocabulary item for you: horrendous.
Let's begin with our review item. Do you remember what horrendous means? ... ... ... ...
Horrendous is an adjective, and it describes something extremely unpleasant, dreadful, or shocking. It suggests something so bad that it causes horror.
...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-18
item.description:
Welcome to your daily vocabulary boost. Today, we're reviewing five words: regime, ensuing, belated, dispel, and ubiquitous.
Let's start with 'regime'. Do you remember what this word means? ... ... ... ... A regime (noun) refers to a system or planned way of doing things, often used for a governmen...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-17
item.description:
Hello, language learners. Today we have fourteen terms to explore: agree to disagree, been on the bubble, hearsay, futile, estranged, god forbid, conflicted, superficial, profound, persuasive, regime, ensuing, belated, and dispel.
Let's start with our review terms.
First up is the phrase, agree to...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-16
item.description:
Welcome to today's lesson. We'll explore eleven vocabulary items: horrendous, agree to disagree, been on the bubble, hearsay, futile, estranged, god forbid, conflicted, superficial, profound, and persuasive.
Let's begin with a review item. Do you remember the meaning of horrendous? ... ... ... ... ...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-15
item.description:
Hello, language learners! Today we have two words for you: ubiquitous and horrendous.
Let's start with a word we've looked at before: ubiquitous. Can you remember what ubiquitous means? ... ... ... ... Ubiquitous describes something that appears to be everywhere, or that is very common and widespre...

item.title: Daily Vocab Lesson: 2026-06-14
item.description:
Hello and welcome. Today, we have one new word to learn: ubiquitous.
Our new word for today is ubiquitous.
Ubiquitous (adjective) means appearing or found everywhere, or very common. Think about something you see absolutely everywhere you go.
For example, "Smartphones are ubiquitous nowadays, eve...
